meta.do_not_edit_reason: This file is the immutable template. Each eval run copies it to tools/eval/harbor/results/<run_id>/sensor-ops-base.json and workers update that copy.
meta.description: TEMPLATE for chaining deploy -> sensor-ops evaluation on the base profile. Harbor has no native task-dependency mechanism (TaskConfig has no depends_on field), so the coordinator encodes the chain as each execution_group's queue_order: [deploy task, sensor-ops task] on the same Brev instance. The sensor-ops task ASSUMES the VSS base stack is already up — enforced by the 'requires_previous_passed' field on each sensor-ops task. If the deploy step fails or is skipped, the sensor-ops step is blocked automatically by the coordinator.
meta.harbor_cmd_template: BREV_INSTANCE=<instance> uvx harbor run --environment-import-path tools.eval.harbor.envs.brev_env:BrevEnvironment -p <dataset_dir> -i <task_id> -a claude-code -n 1 -o <run_dir> --timeout-multiplier 6 --max-retries 0 --model $ANTHROPIC_MODEL --ak api_base=$ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL/v1

coordinator_instructions.overview: You are the coordinator. Your job is NOT to run trials yourself — it is to (1) copy this template to a run-specific path, (2) dispatch ONE worker subagent per execution_group, and (3) aggregate results. Each worker walks its group's queue sequentially on the same Brev instance: deploy task first, then the sensor-ops task that depends on it.

run_directory_protocol.why: This file is a reusable template. Writing progress back into it would pollute it for future runs.
steps: 4. In the COPY, set meta.is_template=false, meta.run_id=<run_id>, meta.run_dir=<RUN_DIR>, meta.started_at=<utcnow>.
5. Pass the COPY's absolute path as progress_file to every worker. Workers read/write ONLY that file, never the template.

1. Validate template: read this file, confirm meta.is_template==true, every task has an execution_group (or status=skipped_*), every group's queue_order references a valid task ID, every sensor-ops task's requires_previous_passed points to a deploy task earlier in the same group.

2. Regenerate the datasets on disk if they're missing (run both commands under meta.regenerate_cmd). The adapters are the source of truth — task.toml / test.sh / instruction.md are generated, not hand-edited.
3. Set up the run directory per run_directory_protocol. progress_file = <RUN_DIR>/sensor-ops-base.json.
4. Ensure .env has ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/BASE_URL/MODEL, NGC_CLI_API_KEY, LLM_REMOTE_URL/MODEL, VLM_REMOTE_URL/MODEL, and (for edge platforms) HF_TOKEN.

5. For each execution_group where status in ('ready','needs_start','needs_provision'): verify the Brev instance exists (brev ls / brev ls nodes) and is RUNNING+READY. Start if STOPPED. Skip the group if unreachable (status='skipped_no_gpu').

6. Dispatch ONE worker subagent per READY group IN PARALLEL (single message, multiple Agent tool calls). Pass each worker: group_id, brev_instance, queue_order (array of task IDs), progress_file (absolute path to the COPY), and the worker_prompt_template text.
7. Workers handle the chain: when a deploy task passes, the paired sensor-ops task is unblocked; when it fails, sensor-ops is marked 'blocked' with a pointer to the failing deploy task.
8. After all workers report back, re-read progress_file and produce a per-group + overall summary.
9. Clean up Brev instances (see cleanup_policy). Stoppable -> brev stop. Non-stoppable (hyperstack/dmz/massedcompute/scaleway) -> brev delete to stop billing. Do NOT touch SPARK (BYOH).

rules: Non-stoppable instances (hyperstack_*, dmz.*, massedcompute_*, scaleway_*): brev delete <name> — they bill 24/7 until deleted.
Stoppable instances (l40s-48gb.2x, g7e.12xlarge): brev stop <name> — keeps disk, cheap to restart.
Do NOT touch pre-existing instances or user-registered external nodes (SPARK).

concurrency_rules: Groups run concurrently (one worker per group, parallel Agent calls).
Within a group, tasks run STRICTLY sequentially — VSS deploys bind the host's ports 8000/3000/30888.
A sensor-ops task MUST run on a host where the matching deploy task just passed — do NOT tear down the VSS stack between the deploy and sensor-ops steps on the same instance.
json_update_protocol: All writes go to the run copy (progress_file), NEVER to tools/eval/harbor/plans/sensor-ops/base.json.
Before a task: status='in_progress', append {started_at, harbor_cmd, result_path} to attempts[].
After a task: status='passed' (reward==1.0) or 'failed' (reward<1.0) or 'blocked' (requires_previous_passed not met).
Atomic write: read -> modify -> write to progress_file + '.tmp' -> rename.
If progress_file's meta.is_template==true, ABORT — coordinator forgot to copy.

meta.worker_prompt_template:
You are a worker subagent for plan 'sensor-ops/base' group '<group_id>' targeting Brev instance '<brev_instance>'. Working dir: /home/ubuntu/video-search-and-summarization. Load env: `set -a; source .env; set +a`.

CRITICAL: progress_file = '<progress_file_abs_path>'. Read and write ONLY this file. If its meta.is_template==true, ABORT — coordinator forgot to copy.

Your queue (run in this order, ONE AT A TIME). Each entry is a task ID that you look up in progress_file.tasks[<id>]:
<queue_order_json>

Per-task workflow:
1. Re-read progress_file. If status=passed, skip. If status=failed with len(attempts)>=3, skip.
2. Check `requires_previous_passed` on this task. If set, look up that other task — if its status != passed, set THIS task status='blocked' with error_notes explaining which predecessor failed, then move on.
3. Atomic update: set status='in_progress', append new attempt {started_at, harbor_cmd, result_path}.
4. If this is a DEPLOY task (skill=='deploy'): do full teardown of any prior VSS stack first (docker rm -f, remove data-dir, git clean). If this is a SENSOR-OPS task (skill=='sensor-ops'): DO NOT tear down — the paired deploy task just left the stack running, and sensor-ops depends on it.
5. Build harbor command from task.dataset_dir + task.task_id and the meta.harbor_cmd_template. Example: `BREV_INSTANCE=<instance> uvx harbor run ... -p <task.dataset_dir> -i <task.task_id> ... -o <run_dir>`.
6. Run harbor in background, tail trial.log + verifier/test-stdout.txt.
7. When it completes, read verifier/reward.txt and verifier/test-stdout.txt. Need reward==1.0 AND test-stdout says 'Results: N passed, 0 failed (of N)'.
8. On pass: status='passed', last_reward=1.0, record result_path.
9. On failure: diagnose (docker logs vss-agent / NIMs / look at agent trajectory). Max 3 attempts per task. After 3 fails: status='failed' with error_notes.
10. Budget 60 min per deploy task, 30 min per sensor-ops task.
11. DO NOT modify skills/ or generate.py or test_base.py unless the bug is there (rare).
12. DO NOT stop/delete the Brev instance (coordinator handles lifecycle). For SPARK (BYOH), NEVER delete.

Report back: per-task summary (status, reward, result_path, issues) + overall pass/fail/blocked count.

A.description: RTX PRO 6000 2-GPU host: deploy base/rtxpro6000bw-shared then run sensor-ops against it

B.description: L40S 2-GPU host: deploy base/l40s-remote-all (no local NIM on 48GB) then sensor-ops

C.description: H100 2-GPU host: deploy base/h100-shared (dmz.h100x2.pcie has driver 580+) then sensor-ops
C.warning: non-stoppable — MUST delete when queue finishes

D.description: DGX-Spark (GB10) BYOH via SSH: deploy base/spark-shared (Edge 4B) then sensor-ops
D.notes: BYOH — leave instance alone in cleanup step. Edge 4B path requires HF_TOKEN.

deploy:rtxpro6000bw-shared.notes: Baseline deploy — LLM+VLM share single RTX PRO 6000.

sensor-ops:rtxpro6000bw.notes: 3 VIOS queries: upload video, snapshot URL at 5s, clip URL 3-5s. All URLs must be Brev secure-link format.

deploy:l40s-remote-all.notes: L40S can't fit 2 NIMs in 48GB; use remote endpoints for both.

sensor-ops:l40s.notes: 3 VIOS queries against the remote-all deployment. VIOS runs locally; only LLM/VLM are remote.

deploy:h100-shared.notes: H100 shared: both NIMs fit on one H100 80GB. Requires dmz.h100x2.pcie (driver 580+).

sensor-ops:h100.notes: 3 VIOS queries. H100 host has lots of VRAM headroom — sensor-ops latency should be lowest of any group.

deploy:spark-shared.notes: DGX-Spark Edge 4B mandatory: standalone vLLM on port 30081 + local VLM NIM. See skills/deploy/references/edge.md.

sensor-ops:spark.notes: 3 VIOS queries on GB10. VST container runs fine on arm64; Edge 4B handles the chat flow.
